summary: Iteration 55 - Phase 3 Patient Flow Board UI. Verified end-to-end against the preview build using admin@ccms.app. The board renders at /scheduling/flow-board with all 6 columns (waiting_room, roomed, ready_for_provider, in_progress, ready_for_checkout, recently_checked_out), correct testids, header counts, and the row card schema (name / status / intake / location / elapsed) — every value rendered as a text label, never color-only. Filters (date, provider, status, intake) and clear-filters all behave correctly. Drove a real today's appointment through the full Phase-2/3 lifecycle (Ready w/ intake-override prompt → Start visit → Ready checkout → Checkout) and confirmed each transition (a) moved the card to the correct column instantly via local merge, (b) refreshed the visible action set, (c) persisted in the DB (verified via GET /api/appointments/{id}). Step-up reauth modal triggered as expected on the first mutating action and was satisfied via 'Use password instead'. Manual Refresh button works; 20s silent polling is wired with visibility check. Patient role is denied — direct nav to /scheduling/flow-board redirects to '/' and the nav-flow-board sidebar entry is not rendered for patients.

issues: current_location_type='roomed' badge appears on a row whose status is now 'ready_for_provider' (column = Ready for Provider). Visually the cell shows two strong context cues (column + ROOMED badge) which is fine, but the column-priority rules in classifyAppointment intentionally keep status above location — consider also updating the Roomed column to use location only when status is checked_in to avoid operator confusion (current code already does this; the location badge just stays sticky on the card after the patient moves to Ready for Provider). Cosmetic only.

critical_code_review_comments: FlowBoardPage.jsx is ~628 lines — still readable but approaching the 700-line threshold. Recommend splitting FlowColumn / FlowRow / FlowRowActions into sibling files under pages/scheduling/flow-board/ for testability.
stageStart() falls back to updated_at in the default branch, which is dead code today (classifyAppointment already excludes any non-matching status) — safe but worth a comment.

The polling effect intentionally re-creates on filter change. That double-invokes when both date and providerId change in the same tick; debouncing isn't strictly needed since fetchBoard guards via setRefreshing, but a small ref-based AbortController would prevent a stale response from clobbering a newer one.

FlowRowActions uses window.prompt / window.confirm for override + no-show. Functional and HIPAA-safe (no PHI exposed), but a shadcn/AlertDialog would make the override-reason flow keyboard-accessible and styleable. Phase-3 review explicitly accepts the prompt — flagged for polish only.
_keepIconRef = UserCheck workaround at the bottom is a code smell; just drop the import if unused.
test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 (PIN dialog → 'Use password instead' on first mutation). Patient denial verified with patient@ccms.app / Patient@ComplianceClinic1.
seed_data_creation: No new seed needed — an existing TESTUnsigned Phase5_5fa032 appointment for today (Dr. Alicia Monroe, 11:45 AM) was already checked-in in the Waiting Room and was driven all the way to Recently Checked Out by this run. Other agents wanting fresh data should re-check-in via the Scheduling day view BookDialog → Check-in.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 Flow Board is green end-to-end. To re-test from scratch: (1) login admin → /scheduling, day view, click an existing appointment → Check-in via BookDialog (step-up via password). (2) Navigate /scheduling/flow-board — the patient appears in Waiting Room. (3) Use flow-action-room / flow-action-ready (intake-override prompt fires if intake_status != completed) / flow-action-start / flow-action-rfco / flow-action-complete / flow-action-checkout / flow-action-depart / flow-action-noshow as per column. (4) Verify DB after each action via GET /api/appointments/{id}. Patient role denial: GET /scheduling/flow-board with patient@ccms.app session redirects to '/'.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues. All 7 review-request behaviours verified.
